strategy_doc_section_loaded: content-strategy-agentic-engineering-101.md — §M1 in detail (lines 260–292: 'Orientation is short, not a setup ramp' at 270), §M3 in detail (line 332: curated skills install at prework, three skills now incl. security-tools per the 2026-05-24 reshape), §Material model (lines 653–669, esp. 665: 'Prework is agentic end-to-end' + 'Pre-fabricating state you could generate in conversation violates ask-the-agent-don't-type-in-a-terminal'), §Mood arc (lines 248–258: prework precedes 'joyful creation' M1 opener), §Recurring themes (line 725 + §Recurring themes block). No standalone 'prework Big Idea' line in the doc; framing is distributed across these anchors and the implicit Big Idea is the file's opening orientation sentence.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract — prework is mood-exempt setup
Lead with discipline, not failure mode
What You'll Learn shape — capability register
Big Idea match — file orientation honors strategy doc
Agentic end-to-end pedagogy fidelity (strategy doc line 665)
M3 boundary — skills installed but not invoked or lectured
Mood framing — joyful-creation entry primed without resolution
Repo-choice criteria match strategy doc §Delivery architecture
Three-skill install (incl. security-tools) consistent with strategy doc M3 anchor + artefact contract
Real-work-only requirement honored — four task sizes named, not toy data

notes: Re-audit at 2026-05-24 against reshaped prework (Step 0→2 collapse, new Step 5 screen-and-ready prompt, security-tools added to install set, four-task-sizes framing pulled forward into 'What to bring'). Prework is a setup artefact, not a numbered module — Module File Shape's Big Idea / Key Concepts / What You'll Learn sections do not apply (§§5/6/7 N/A). Strategy doc carries no dedicated 'prework Big Idea' line; the implicit Big Idea ('Land at Module 1 with a Claude Code session started in the repo you're going to grow, the curated skills installed, and one trivial bug picked') is the file's opening orientation sentence and matches strategy-doc lines 270 + 665 + 332. Mood is setup-exempt per §1's diagnostic: every step is plumbing (pick repo, open session, fetch tarball, extract+install, pick a bug). Strategy-fidelity §4 is the load-bearing gate: file installs the three curated skills (access-control-analysis + stride + security-tools) WITHOUT invoking them or lecturing security — preserves M3's earn-the-trust teaching moment; the security-tools rick-roll cover at M3 (per maintainer block line 88 + artefact contract) is not spoiled here. The new Step 5 ('Pick a trivial bug and confirm M1 readiness') honors strategy doc line 270 ('M1 starts in the chat with the repo open') by pre-staging the picked bug — the bug-finding conversation happens at prework per line 665 ('Claude runs the repo-choice conversation, the bug-finding conversation, the install check'). The four-task-sizes framing in 'What to bring' (trivial bug / small multi-file / small feature / real send-off task) maps cleanly to M1 / M2 / M3 / M4-onward and matches the strategy doc's per-module artifact table (lines 235–240) without front-running any module's teaching moment — students bring raw material; the modules teach what to do with it. Engineer-register peer voice ('Your call.', 'Pick one.', 'A picked bug from Step 5. Write it down, file/line, or a one-sentence summary in a note.') matches AE101 voice quintet. Closing Connections question ('what's one trick you figured out with Claude Code that nobody taught you?') primes M1's joyful-creation mood without resolving it. Maintainer block discloses CONTENT_URL placeholder as deliberate carve-out to check_prompts.md §1 (build-time substitution) — not a strategy concern. No blocking findings; no TODOs.
